mission.title: Install and Configure Thanos on Kubernetes
mission.description: Thanos is a highly available metrics system that extends existing Prometheus deployments with long-term storage, global query view, and unlimited retention across multiple clusters. Installing Thanos allows platform teams to aggregate and query metrics from many Prometheus instances in a single, horizontally scalable system.

steps.title: Add the Bitnami Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Bitnami Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration to access the Thanos chart.
```bash
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Thanos using Helm
steps.description:
Install Thanos in a new namespace called 'thanos' using the Bitnami chart.
```bash
helm install thanos bitnami/thanos --version 17.4.1 --namespace thanos --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Thanos component pods (query, query-frontend, compactor, storegateway) are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace thanos
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Thanos query component to ensure it runs efficiently under load.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment thanos-query -n thanos -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"query","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"1Gi"}}}]}}}}'
```

steps.title: Access the Thanos Query UI
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access the Thanos Query UI from your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/thanos-query-frontend --namespace thanos 9090:9090
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Thanos Helm release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall thanos --namespace thanos
```

uninstall.title: Clean up persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any persistent volume claims (PVCs) created by the Thanos compactor and store gateway components.
```bash
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace thanos
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'thanos' namespace to remove all remaining resources and verify the removal.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace thanos
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current Helm values and resources for Thanos before upgrading.
```bash
helm get values thanos --namespace thanos > thanos-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Make sure the Bitnami Helm repository is up to date with the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Thanos
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Thanos installation to the latest chart version.
```bash
helm upgrade thanos bitnami/thanos --namespace thanos --version 17.4.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Thanos pods are running the new version successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace thanos
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If Thanos component pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for configuration errors or missing object storage credentials.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace thanos
```
A common cause is a missing or malformed objstore.yml secret required by the compactor, storegateway, and sidecar components.

troubleshooting.title: Query UI not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the Thanos Query UI is not accessible, verify the service is running and the port forward targets the correct service.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace thanos
```

troubleshooting.title: No metrics from existing Prometheus
troubleshooting.description:
If queries return no data, ensure the Thanos sidecar is deployed alongside each Prometheus instance and is discoverable by the query component. Check the configured store endpoints.
```bash
kubectl exec -n thanos deploy/thanos-query -- wget -qO- http://localhost:10902/api/v1/stores
```

troubleshooting.title: High memory usage in compactor
troubleshooting.description:
If the Thanos compactor is consuming excessive memory, adjust its resource limits. Check current usage first.
```bash
kubectl top pods --namespace thanos
```
Then update the limits through Helm values or by editing the statefulset.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Thanos will have all component pods (query, query-frontend, compactor, storegateway) running in the 'thanos' namespace, and you will be able to access the Thanos Query UI at http://localhost:9090. Resource limits should be set appropriately and object storage should be configured to enable long-term metric retention.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine. For production use, you will also need an object storage bucket (such as S3, GCS, or MinIO) to enable long-term metric retention.
